message:
test(charmed): add comprehensive E2E navigation flow tests (bd-2zj3)

Navigation Flow E2E Tests:
- Add application startup tests verifying dashboard renders initially
- Add sidebar verification tests for all 8 pages
- Add status bar theme display tests
- Add help overlay accessibility tests (? key, Escape to close)

Page Navigation Tests:
- Add keyboard shortcut navigation tests (1-8 keys)
- Add Tab/Shift+Tab navigation tests
- Add Enter key selection tests
- Add navigation sequence tests (Dashboard → Services → Jobs → etc.)

Theme Switching Tests:
- Add theme toggle tests (t key)
- Add theme cycling tests (Dark → Light → Dracula → Solarized → etc.)
- Add theme persistence during navigation tests

App Module Improvements:
- Clean up app.rs for better test integration
- Update main.rs for improved startup flow

The navigation tests use the E2ERunner framework to simulate user
interactions and verify the TUI responds correctly to keyboard input,
ensuring consistent navigation behavior across all pages and themes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3eru","title":"Unit tests: filtering engines (jobs/logs/docs)","description":"Add focused unit tests for filtering/search logic (without spinning up the full UI).

Why:
- Filtering and search are easy to subtly break (case sensitivity, tokenization, pagination interactions).
- We want fast feedback during refactors.

Coverage:
- Jobs filtering/sorting:
  - query matches by name/id/service
  - status filters compose correctly
  - sorting is stable and deterministic
- Logs filtering:
  - level/service selectors work
  - free-text query matches expected fields
- Docs search:
  - match finding and next/prev navigation over a rendered buffer

Edge cases to include:
- empty query
- Unicode input
- very long query (no panic, bounded behavior)
- paste-like input containing newlines/whitespace

Nice-to-have:
- Add a small set of property tests (e.g., filter is idempotent, sorting preserves multiset) if it stays lightweight.

Acceptance:
- Core filter logic is correct and stays correct during refactors.
- Tests are deterministic and run fast.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.169792772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:33:26.390328143Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:33:26.390262520Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-1fxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:26.759858058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-212j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:25.449597433Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:28.296789159Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.182594415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3gt6","title":"Quality: README section for demo_showcase","description":"Document how to run (and trust) the showcase.

Include:
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` basics
- flags: theme/seed/no-animations/no-color/no-mouse/no-alt-screen, ssh mode, self-check
- keybindings summary + discoverability notes (help overlay, command palette if present)
- E2E instructions:
  - how to run the smoke tour and where artifacts/logs are written
  - how to run a single scenario for debugging

Also include a *feature coverage matrix* (self-contained):
- For each crate feature we claim (bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour/huh/wish/charmed_log/harmonica), list:
  - where it appears in the UI (page/workflow)
  - which automated test(s) cover it (unit/e2e/snapshot)
  - any manual-only checks (mouse drag, SSH interactive)

Acceptance:
- Someone can run the demo and understand it without reading code.
- Someone can validate "everything works" without rediscovering the plan.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.297365816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:34:49.959313310Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:34:49.959246495Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:31.956437980Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1knu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:34.482189415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.313191791Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3if6","title":"Implement wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit)","description":"Implement Wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit) matching Go wish.

Scope:
- Define middleware trait/type in crates/wish to compose handlers.
- Implement standard middleware: logging, recover/panic handling, rate limiting.
- Ensure middleware order and error propagation match Go semantics.
- Add unit tests and integration tests using wish examples.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Middleware API supports chaining and is used by default server builder.
- Logging/recover/ratelimit middleware behave as expected with tests.
- Integration test demonstrates middleware in a sample server.","notes":"Files: crates/wish/src/lib.rs (or middleware module), crates/wish/examples/*. Use legacy_wish for reference.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:28.687094198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:47:14.493720286Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:47:14.493694748Z","close_reason":"Middleware pattern already fully implemented: logging, recover, ratelimiter, accesscontrol, activeterm, elapsed, comment - all matching Go wish","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-3ilg","title":"E2E Tests: Shell-out and terminal restore","description":"End-to-end tests for terminal release and restore (shell-out).

## Purpose
Test the signature 'drop to shell' feature that releases
the terminal for external commands and restores the TUI.

## Test Requirements

### Diagnostics Pager (D key)
- [ ] Press 'D' triggers pager open
- [ ] Terminal released to pager
- [ ] Diagnostics content displayed
- [ ] Pager exit restores TUI
- [ ] TUI state intact after restore

### Terminal State
- [ ] Alternate screen buffer exit
- [ ] Cursor visibility restored
- [ ] Mouse capture released
- [ ] Raw mode disabled during shell

### Error Handling
- [ ] Pager not found handled gracefully
- [ ] Pager crash doesn't corrupt TUI
- [ ] Ctrl+C during pager handled

### Headless Mode
- [ ] Headless mode skips pager
- [ ] Notification shown instead
- [ ] CI environment detection

## Test Approach
Use PTY to capture terminal state transitions.
Verify screen buffer and mode changes.

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log terminal mode before release
- [ ] Log pager command and exit code
- [ ] Log terminal mode after restore
- [ ] Capture screen before/after

## Success Criteria
- Clean terminal release and restore
- No terminal corruption possible
- Works with common pagers (less, more)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:08.607752071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:45.929987964Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","shell-out","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ilg","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:08.642392046Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ilg","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:08.861928305Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ilg","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:45.929961194Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3j9x","title":"demo_showcase: core TUI shell + router (bubbletea)","description":"Implement the reusable application shell that everything else plugs into.

Key ideas:
- One top-level App model that owns routing + global concerns (theme, status, notifications).
- Pages are sub-models/components; App delegates update/view and composes commands.
- Strong separation between domain data (fake backend) and presentation.

Acceptance:
- App boots into a multi-pane layout with navigation and at least one page.
- Clean message routing (no spaghetti downcasts everywhere).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.314919933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333853721Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j9x","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333820659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3jg2","title":"Interactions: bracketed paste support in inputs/search","description":"Bubbletea delivers paste sequences as `KeyMsg` with `paste=true` (and/or via terminal paste events).

Why:
- Paste is a "real app" interaction: users paste queries, IDs, paths, and commit hashes.
- Terminals can deliver large paste payloads; naïve handling can lag or break inputs.

Ensure paste works sensibly in:
- Jobs query bar
- Logs query/filter input
- Docs search
- Wizard text fields (huh)
- Notes scratchpad (textarea)

Edge cases:
- Very large paste:
  - should not freeze the UI
  - inputs should apply paste in a bounded way (e.g., respect char limits)
- Multi-line paste:
  - for search inputs: collapse whitespace/newlines into spaces (or clearly defined behavior)
  - for textarea: preserve newlines

Acceptance:
- Pasting a multi-word query "just works" across inputs.
- No hangs or panics with large paste payloads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.767936662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:16.372890345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:16.372758850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","paste"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.786648712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:58.203351507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ju1","title":"charmed_log: Thread Safety & Reliability Hardening","description":"# Epic: charmed_log Thread Safety & Reliability Hardening

## Background
The charmed_log crate provides structured logging with lipgloss styling. During deep review,
several critical issues were identified that can cause application crashes or silent data loss.

## Critical Issues Identified

### 1. Lock Poisoning on RwLock::unwrap() - CRITICAL
**Location**: Lines 427, 468, 475, 481, 488, 494, 500, 506, 512, 518, 528, 579, 597
**Problem**: Every read/write lock acquisition uses .unwrap() which panics if lock is poisoned.
Lock poisoning occurs when a thread panics while holding a lock.
**Impact**: A single panic in any formatting routine breaks ALL future logging in the application.
**Risk**: Production reliability - one panic → application crash cascade

### 2. Silent I/O Error Discarding - CRITICAL
**Location**: Line 598: `let _ = inner.writer.write_all(output.as_bytes());`
**Problem**: Write failures to stderr go completely unnoticed (disk full, permission denied, pipe closed).
**Impact**: Logs silently disappear without any notification.
**Risk**: Silent data loss in production - critical audit logs may vanish

### 3. Backtrace Overhead in Caller Reporting - HIGH
**Location**: Line 308: `let bt = Backtrace::new();`
**Problem**: Every log call with report_caller=true captures and parses full stack trace.
**Impact**: 100x+ slower than regular logging when caller reporting is enabled.
**Risk**: Severe performance degradation if accidentally enabled in production

### 4. Double Lock Pattern Race Window - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 579-598
**Problem**: log() acquires read lock, formats, drops, then acquires write lock.
Configuration could change between the two lock acquisitions.
**Impact**: Possible inconsistency in output formatting under concurrent configuration changes.

## Design Philosophy for Fixes
- Logging should be resilient - never crash the application
- Errors should be reported through a configurable error handler
- Performance-critical paths should have explicit opt-in for expensive features
- Thread safety should use recover-from-poisoning strategy for logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All lock acquisitions handle poisoning gracefully
- [ ] I/O errors are propagated to configurable error handler
- [ ] Backtrace overhead is documented with performance warning
- [ ] All changes have regression tests
- [ ] Zero clippy warnings","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:10.326116387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:22.270057599Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:22.269990013Z","close_reason":"All child beads complete: bd-3lwk (error handler), bd-1bja (lock poisoning), bd-2go9 (caller overhead docs), bd-3lk7 (double-lock fix). charmed_log now has robust thread safety and reliability.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","critical","thread-safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ju1","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:10.342713910Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3mh2","title":"demo_showcase theme: semantic tokens + shared style helpers","description":"Build the styling foundation for the entire app.

Implement:
- A theme module that maps lipgloss `ThemeContext` (ColorSlots) into semantic UI primitives.
- Shared helpers: `card()`, `panel()`, `badge()`, `button()`, `table_style()`, `modal()`, `status_bar()`, etc.

Guidelines:
- No ad-hoc hex strings scattered across pages.
- Prefer semantic roles (primary/success/warn/error/muted) and allow theme switching.

Acceptance:
- A page can be built using only shared style helpers + minimal page-specific overrides.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:23.252512028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:37:33.002106933Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:37:33.002041060Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-2ucj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:32.937693109Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:23.270859361Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:31.724599289Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mtt","title":"demo_showcase core: command palette (search + actions)","description":"Optional but very compelling polish feature: a command palette ("quick actions") that makes the app feel modern.

Why:
- It dramatically improves discoverability and speed for power users.
- It showcases composing bubbles components (textinput + list) into a cohesive UX.

UX:
- Trigger:
  - `/` (or another agreed key) opens the palette
  - `esc` closes
- Layout:
  - text input at top
  - filtered list of commands below
- Behavior:
  - fuzzy-ish matching over command names + keywords
  - enter runs selected action
  - shows short descriptions and shortcuts (optional)

Actions to include (minimum):
- Navigation:
  - jump to Dashboard/Jobs/Docs/Wizard/Files/Logs/Settings
- Toggles:
  - toggle theme picker / cycle theme
  - toggle no-color / reduce-motion
- Utilities:
  - export current view (plain/HTML)
  - open diagnostics (pager / export)

Implementation guidance:
- Maintain a small registry of commands (id, title, keywords, handler).
- Handlers should emit bubbletea Cmds / messages (no direct side effects in UI code).

Acceptance:
- Palette is keyboard-friendly, fast, and feels integrated (not a bolted-on demo).
- Actions are safe in headless/self-check mode (no hangs).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.480166189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:32:37.827452925Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:26.750440223Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:28.035096998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.499012063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mxk","title":"Jobs: progress visualization polish","description":"Polish job progress visualization so it looks like a modern CI dashboard (compelling, readable, information-dense).

Why:
- Progress UI is where “toy” demos instantly reveal themselves.
- This task should make the Jobs page feel premium even in a plain terminal.

Requirements:
- Use `bubbles::progress` for determinate progress:
  - clear fill, percentage (optional), and label
  - themed colors that match status (running/success/fail/cancel)
- Use spinners for indeterminate states:
  - e.g., “waiting”, “queued”, “uploading”, “finalizing”
- Status clarity:
  - icons/chips for state
  - duration + ETA-ish hint when possible
  - differentiate “stuck” vs “slow” (e.g., warn color after threshold)
- Visual polish:
  - consistent spacing and alignment across rows
  - good behavior in narrow widths (truncate/wrap rules)
  - respects `--no-color` (still readable) and `--no-animations`

Optional (nice if it’s easy):
- Stage-based progress (build/test/deploy) with per-stage completion markers.

Acceptance:
- Looks like a real CI dashboard.
- Clear at a glance: what’s running, what’s blocked, what failed, what finished.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - rendering decisions given job state (spinner vs bar, colors/labels)
  - width-constrained rendering produces stable output
- E2E:
  - wizard -> jobs scenario should assert at least one running job shows an updating progress indicator.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.217085325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:43:57.767439291Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","progress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.232965048Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:56.844461854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:59.582343606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3nit","title":"E2E Tests: demo_showcase wizard workflow","description":"End-to-end tests for demo_showcase deployment wizard workflow.

## Purpose
Test the complete multi-step wizard deployment flow,
including validation, navigation, and completion.

## Test Requirements

### Wizard Flow
- [ ] Start wizard from page 7
- [ ] Step 1: Service name entry and validation
- [ ] Step 2: Service type selection
- [ ] Step 3: Environment selection
- [ ] Step 4: Environment variables
- [ ] Step 5: Review and confirmation
- [ ] Deployment progress display
- [ ] Completion notification

### Validation
- [ ] Empty service name rejected
- [ ] Invalid characters rejected
- [ ] Required selections enforced
- [ ] Clear error messages displayed

### Navigation
- [ ] Next/previous step buttons work
- [ ] Can go back and modify earlier steps
- [ ] Cancel abandons wizard
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work

### Integration
- [ ] Deployment triggers job creation
- [ ] Job visible in Jobs page
- [ ] Logs generated in Logs page
- [ ] Dashboard metrics update

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log each wizard step transition
- [ ] Log validation attempts/results
- [ ] Log deployment progress events
- [ ] Full form state at submission

## Success Criteria
- Complete wizard flow works E2E
- Validation prevents bad input
- Deployment integrates across pages","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:41.429691632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:37:23.698350655Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nit","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:41.456013478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nit","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:05.929237471Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nit","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:40.423566914Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3nrc","title":"E2E: CLI script wrapper for CI + local debugging","description":"Add a human-friendly script that runs the *smoke* E2E suite with good logs.

Deliverables:
- `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (or similar) that:
  - sets deterministic env (seed, no-color optional)
  - runs `cargo test -p demo_showcase` with clear output
  - on failure, prints where artifacts were written

Notes:
- The full scenario suite is added by a follow-up task so we get early value.

Acceptance:

 {"id":"bd-3nyf","title":"EPIC: Integration Test Coverage","description":"Integration tests that verify cross-crate behavior and real terminal interaction.

- Test how crates work together
- Test actual terminal I/O where possible
- Verify ANSI escape sequence correctness

## Key Integration Points
1. bubbletea + bubbles - components in event loop
2. bubbletea + lipgloss - styled output rendering
3. glamour + lipgloss - markdown with styles
4. huh + bubbletea - form in TUI context
5. charmed_log + any - logging integration

## Test Infrastructure Needs
- Terminal emulator/PTY test harness
- ANSI sequence capture and verification
- Cross-platform terminal capability detection
- Timing-sensitive test handling","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:41.653513760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:41.663266853Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nyf","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:41.663223292Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-665u","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage Suite","description":"Master epic for achieving comprehensive test coverage across all charmed_rust crates.

## Goals
- Real tests only - NO mocks, fakes, or stubs
- Direct integration with actual terminal/TTY behavior
- Property-based testing for algorithmic/numeric code
- E2E tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Sub-Epics
1. Unit Test Coverage - expand tests in every crate
2. Integration Test Coverage - cross-crate and terminal tests
3. E2E Test Coverage - full application workflows
4. Property Test Coverage - proptest for edge cases

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs have at least one test
- Integration tests verify cross-crate behavior
- E2E tests cover critical user workflows
- Property tests catch edge cases in numeric/algorithmic code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:17.362248783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:24.179013006Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-665u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ARCHITECTURE NOTE: This test coverage effort is structured in 4 parallel tracks:

1. **Unit Tests (bd-28co)** - Foundation layer, can start immediately
   - Tests individual functions/methods in isolation
   - NO mocks - use real implementations
   - Priority: Start with bubbletea and lipgloss (most used)

2. **Integration Tests (bd-3nyf)** - Depends on unit tests
   - Tests cross-crate interactions
   - Includes terminal I/O verification
   - Requires PTY test harness

3. **E2E Tests (bd-1cgb)** - Depends on unit + infrastructure
   - Tests complete user workflows
   - Requires logging framework first (bd-28kk)
   - Focus on demo_showcase application

4. **Property Tests (bd-ndn1)** - Parallel with unit tests
   - Uses proptest for random input generation
   - Focus on numeric/algorithmic code
   - Lower priority but high value for edge cases

RECOMMENDED EXECUTION ORDER:
1. Build E2E logging infrastructure (bd-28kk) - unblocks all E2E work
2. Start unit tests for core crates (bubbletea, lipgloss)
3. Add integration tests as unit tests complete
4. Run E2E tests on demo_showcase
5. Add property tests for regression prevention","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6wkl","title":"E2E scenario: mouse scroll + click-to-select","description":"Scenario: validate mouse scroll + click-to-select/focus behavior.

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium feature and easy to regress.
- This scenario proves bubbletea mouse plumbing + demo_showcase hit-testing/mapping is correct in a headless, deterministic way.

Steps (suggested):
- Start the app with mouse enabled.
- Navigate to a scrollable screen (Docs viewport or Files preview).
- Inject mouse wheel events to scroll down, then up.
- Navigate to a clickable/selection surface (Dashboard card grid or Jobs table).
- Inject a mouse click at known coordinates and assert selection/focus changes.
- (Optional) Toggle mouse off in Settings and assert wheel/click no longer changes state.

Assertions:
- Scroll wheel changes viewport offset (rendered output differs and/or a scroll indicator updates).
- Click selects the intended item (visible highlight/focus state changes deterministically).
- No panics; no runaway input loops.

Implementation notes:
- Prefer the E2E runner’s semantic DSL events (`MouseMsg`) for stability.
- If needed, add a “raw byte” mode sub-test that injects an xterm SGR mouse sequence to exercise the InputParser.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in mouse mapping and makes failures actionable (step id + last frame + artifact path).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.031885548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100709336Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","mouse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:17.148362656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:21.100671155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:19.756678314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:18.467597486Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6wkl","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:50:58.052269553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7jxu","title":"demo_showcase theme: polished UI components (tabs, chips, banners)","description":"Build higher-level styled UI primitives so pages can be assembled like a design system.

Why:
- The demo needs a cohesive, premium visual language.
- Centralizing these patterns prevents ad-hoc styling and keeps pages consistent.

Components to provide (examples):
- Tabs/pills for sub-sections
- Chips/tags for status (ok/warn/error/running)
- Alert banners (info/warn/error) with icons and optional actions
- Empty states (icon + title + guidance)
- Progress-with-label layouts
- Small "stat" widgets (label + value + delta)

Design constraints:
- Must work in:
  - truecolor and 256-color
  - no-color/ASCII mode (fallback styles)
  - narrow terminals (components truncate gracefully)
- Components should be usable from any page with minimal configuration.

Testability:
- Components should be snapshot-testable (render a small representative example set).

Acceptance:
- Pages can assemble these like a cohesive design system.
- UI feels consistent and premium across Dashboard/Jobs/Logs/Docs/Wizard.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:25.396250192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:10.479515924Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:10.479451744Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7jxu","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:25.411568884Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-7jxu","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:34.170348451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8wlh","title":"glamour: Table Rendering & Markdown Hardening","description":"# Epic: glamour Table Rendering & Markdown Hardening

## Background
The glamour crate renders Markdown to styled terminal output. Deep review identified
issues in table rendering and ANSI handling that affect output quality.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Table Truncation Now ANSI-Aware - FIXED ✓
**Location**: table.rs lines 720-733
**Status**: Fixed in this review session - truncate_content() now properly handles
ANSI escape sequences and adds reset before ellipsis.

### 2. Style Cache Unbounded Growth - MEDIUM
**Location**: syntax.rs lines 568-625
**Problem**: StyleCache uses Vec with linear search and no size limit.
Large code blocks with many styles could consume significant memory.
**Impact**: Memory growth with large documents.

### 3. Language Alias Validation Missing - LOW
**Location**: lib.rs lines 623-630
**Problem**: No validation that target language exists or that aliases don't form cycles.
**Impact**: Silent failures when using invalid aliases.

### 4. Width Calculation Edge Cases - LOW
**Location**: table.rs lines 505-550
**Problem**: If max_table_width is very small (less than overhead), columns collapse to 0.
No validation that min_width * columns <= max_width.
**Impact**: Malformed output with very narrow terminals.

## Design Philosophy
- Graceful degradation for edge cases
- Bounded resource usage
- Defensive validation of configuration
- Clear error messages for invalid input

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Style cache has configurable size limit
- [ ] Language aliases validated on registration
- [ ] Table width calculation handles edge cases
- [ ] All changes have tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:24.212702064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:07:17.857828709Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:07:17.857763157Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","markdown","rendering","table"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8wlh","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:24.232680489Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
